audience: VTJ staff handling repeated shared-inbox triage and customer replies
product_feel: Gmail-speed workbench with Front/Help Scout style shared-inbox ownership and Superhuman-style action clarity
design_principles: Expose operational state before action: environment, read-only/write, production readiness, and selected queue must be visible without opening drawers.
Make queues scannable: store/channel, assignee, and status counts should read like stable work lists, not hidden filters.
Protect irreversible actions: reply send surfaces must show identity, recipient, body readiness, and blockers near the send buttons.
Keep density high and decoration low: compact rows, restrained color, stable controls, and no marketing-style panels.
borrowed_patterns: Gmail: persistent search, keyboard-first toolbar, preview-pane list/detail rhythm.
Front: shared inbox ownership, assignment load, channel queues.
Help Scout: saved views and tags as operational filters.
Superhuman: quick action confidence, command palette affordance, and explicit shortcut hints.
must_not_do: Do not mutate GitHub or Google Sheets.
Do not add decorative gradients, hero layouts, or card-heavy marketing UI.
